rows.name: Standing Light Punch
rows.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; Run~Stop cancel: -14/-19

raw.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; Run~Stop cancel: -14/-19

rows.name: Standing Medium Punch
rows.range_notes: Short range but strong 5f punish starter if it reaches
rows.notes: Easily hitconfirmable into Target Combo; farthest ~40% of hitbox is shifted upward (can whiff against some crouching attack hurtboxes in whiff punish scenarios); extends a forward leg hurtbox until end of recovery; Run~Stop cancel: -6/-12

raw.rangeNotes: Short range but strong 5f punish starter if it reaches
raw.notes: Easily hitconfirmable into Target Combo; farthest ~40% of hitbox is shifted upward (can whiff against some crouching attack hurtboxes in whiff punish scenarios); extends a forward leg hurtbox until end of recovery; Run~Stop cancel: -6/-12

rows.name: Standing Heavy Punch
rows.notes: Great poke in neutral; large number of active frames make it a great meaty; when used out of Drive Rush, links into 2MK on hit or gives pressure on block; Run~Stop cancel: -3/-8

raw.notes: Great poke in neutral; large number of active frames make it a great meaty; when used out of Drive Rush, links into 2MK on hit or gives pressure on block; Run~Stop cancel: -3/-8

rows.name: Standing Light Kick
rows.notes: Incredible range for a light normal and a great whiff punish; Run~Stop cancel: -14/-16; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

raw.notes: Incredible range for a light normal and a great whiff punish; Run~Stop cancel: -14/-16; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

rows.name: Standing Medium Kick
rows.notes: Has good forward movement; whiffs on crouching opponents at farther ranges unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up makes it strong in neutral; Super is much harder to hitconfirm but can buffer into a safe SA2 in neutral; front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 4-15, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

raw.notes: Has good forward movement; whiffs on crouching opponents at farther ranges unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up makes it strong in neutral; Super is much harder to hitconfirm but can buffer into a safe SA2 in neutral; front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 4-15, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

rows.name: Standing Heavy Kick
rows.notes: Excellent ranged poke and whiff punish tool; even at max range, can convert Punish Counter spinning juggle state into 623KK or Drive Rush 5HP juggles; 2 extra recovery frames on whiff; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

raw.notes: Excellent ranged poke and whiff punish tool; even at max range, can convert Punish Counter spinning juggle state into 623KK or Drive Rush 5HP juggles; 2 extra recovery frames on whiff; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

rows.name: Crouching Light Punch
rows.notes: Primary combo starter; chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; Run~Stop cancel: -12/-18; DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

raw.notes: Primary combo starter; chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; Run~Stop cancel: -12/-18; DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

rows.name: Crouching Medium Punch
rows.notes: Good buffer tool and great for pressure out of Drive Rush; Run~Stop cancel: -8/-11; 2f extra recovery on whiff and extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-8 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

raw.notes: Good buffer tool and great for pressure out of Drive Rush; Run~Stop cancel: -8/-11; 2f extra recovery on whiff and extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-8 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

rows.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
rows.notes: Forces stand; decent anti-air (cannot hit cross-up); only first 2 active frames are cancelable; cancel into 236HK on block leaves a 5f gap and is DI-safe; Run~Stop cancel: +3/-7

raw.notes: Forces stand; decent anti-air (cannot hit cross-up); only first 2 active frames are cancelable; cancel into 236HK on block leaves a 5f gap and is DI-safe; Run~Stop cancel: +3/-7

rows.name: Crouching Light Kick
rows.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK

raw.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK

rows.name: Crouching Medium Kick
rows.notes: Run~Stop cancel: -8/-12; Range: 1.46; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

raw.notes: Run~Stop cancel: -8/-12; Range: 1.46; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

rows.name: Low Spinning Sweep
rows.notes: Has juggle potential; on block, always plays out a fixed 27f recovery (cannot be made safer with meaty timing)

raw.notes: Has juggle potential; on block, always plays out a fixed 27f recovery (cannot be made safer with meaty timing)

rows.name: Jumping Medium Punch
rows.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; cancels to j.214K with a long cancel window; lower body projectile invuln 6-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; cancels to j.214K with a long cancel window; lower body projectile invuln 6-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
rows.notes: Spike Knockdown on counter-hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; great downward hitbox for close jump-ins and neutral jump throw baits; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Spike Knockdown on counter-hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; great downward hitbox for close jump-ins and neutral jump throw baits; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Jumping Light Kick
rows.notes: Cross-up

raw.notes: Cross-up

rows.name: Jumping Medium Kick
rows.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
rows.notes: Great horizontal range for a jump-in; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Great horizontal range for a jump-in; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Neutral Jumping Heavy Kick
rows.notes: Strong and fast air-to-air; lower body projectile invuln 6-19f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Strong and fast air-to-air; lower body projectile invuln 6-19f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Chin Buster
rows.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; Run~Stop cancel: KD+30/-12

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; Run~Stop cancel: KD+30/-12

rows.name: Triple Flash Kicks 1
rows.notes: (refers to scaled damage); great forward movement, will combo even if 5MK hits an extended limb hurtbox; true blockstring even at max delay

raw.notes: (refers to scaled damage); great forward movement, will combo even if 5MK hits an extended limb hurtbox; true blockstring even at max delay

rows.name: Triple Flash Kicks 2
rows.notes: (refers to scaled damage); 2f gap between final 2 hits on block; whiffs on crouch blocking opponents (much more unsafe); 2f worse KD Adv. if only the final hit connects

raw.notes: (refers to scaled damage); 2f gap between final 2 hits on block; whiffs on crouch blocking opponents (much more unsafe); 2f worse KD Adv. if only the final hit connects

rows.name: Knee Strikes
rows.notes: Can walk forward for a corner throw loop with 1f timing vs. standard size characters (2f vs. Marisa, 3f vs. Blanka/E.Honda, 4f vs. Zangief); can also Drive Rush for easy manual timing (can whiff DR~5LP to bait 5f reversals, but is punished by reversal 4f lights); only +1 after forward dash (can pressure with jabs, but throw loses to 4f); can use Drive Rush meaty normals after a midscreen throw; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

raw.notes: Can walk forward for a corner throw loop with 1f timing vs. standard size characters (2f vs. Marisa, 3f vs. Blanka/E.Honda, 4f vs. Zangief); can also Drive Rush for easy manual timing (can whiff DR~5LP to bait 5f reversals, but is punished by reversal 4f lights); only +1 after forward dash (can pressure with jabs, but throw loses to 4f); can use Drive Rush meaty normals after a midscreen throw; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

rows.name: Hell Wheel
rows.notes: Side switches, has great corner carry; does not allow throw/strike oki after back throwing opponent into corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

raw.notes: Side switches, has great corner carry; does not allow throw/strike oki after back throwing opponent into corner; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

rows.name: Flame Strike
rows.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Impact|Drive Impact]]. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

rows.name: Lightning Crack (Block)
rows.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see Drive Reversal.

raw.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

rows.name: Lightning Crack (Recovery)
rows.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see Drive Reversal.

raw.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

rows.name: Drive Parry
rows.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Parry|Drive Parry]]. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

rows.name: Parry Drive Rush
rows.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.745 (min/throw), 2.449 (min/block), 3.721 (max/final DR frame)

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.745 (min/throw), 2.449 (min/block), 3.721 (max/final DR frame)

rows.name: Drive Rush Cancel
rows.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

rows.name: Hadoken
rows.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; useful for meaty corner setups if well-spaced, preventing wakeup SA1

raw.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; useful for meaty corner setups if well-spaced, preventing wakeup SA1

rows.name: Hadoken
rows.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

raw.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

rows.name: Hadoken
rows.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

raw.notes: 1-hit projectile; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

rows.name: Hadoken
rows.notes: 2-hit OD projectile with extremely fast recovery; at some ranges, Ken can follow up with Drive Rush to combo afterwards; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

raw.notes: 2-hit OD projectile with extremely fast recovery; at some ranges, Ken can follow up with Drive Rush to combo afterwards; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

rows.name: Shoryuken
rows.notes: Good anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; reduced damage on high anti-air connect (active frames 3-10)

raw.notes: Good anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; reduced damage on high anti-air connect (active frames 3-10)

rows.name: Shoryuken
rows.notes: Good anti-air; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Good anti-air; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Shoryuken
rows.notes: Good anti-air; 3rd hit whiffs vs. crouch block (2f worse advantage, 75 less chip dmg); cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Good anti-air; 3rd hit whiffs vs. crouch block (2f worse advantage, 75 less chip dmg); cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Shoryuken
rows.notes: All hits are a true blockstring; Ken's best reversal option; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: All hits are a true blockstring; Ken's best reversal option; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
rows.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state if 1st hit connects; if the first hit whiffs the second hit will always do 700 damage, but has worse KD Adv.; both hits connect on crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state if 1st hit connects; if the first hit whiffs the second hit will always do 700 damage, but has worse KD Adv.; both hits connect on crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
rows.notes: Better KD Adv. if first hit connects; both hits connect on crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Better KD Adv. if first hit connects; both hits connect on crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
rows.notes: 2nd and 3rd hits whiff on crouching opponents (unsafe on crouching hit or block); same knockdown advantage regardless of which hit initially connects; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: 2nd and 3rd hits whiff on crouching opponents (unsafe on crouching hit or block); same knockdown advantage regardless of which hit initially connects; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
rows.notes: Great range and speed, useful in whiff punishes and juggles; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Great range and speed, useful in whiff punishes and juggles; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
rows.notes: Forward jump only; can hit cross-up; puts opponent into limited juggle state; the last 11f of recovery puts Ken in a crouching state

raw.notes: Forward jump only; can hit cross-up; puts opponent into limited juggle state; the last 11f of recovery puts Ken in a crouching state

rows.name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
rows.notes: Forward jump only; 7f extra landing recovery on whiff; can hit twice vs. airborne opponents; can hit cross-up (deals 5f less hitstun, 200 less damage); Ken is in a crouching state from 4th landing recovery frame onward

raw.notes: Forward jump only; 7f extra landing recovery on whiff; can hit twice vs. airborne opponents; can hit cross-up (deals 5f less hitstun, 200 less damage); Ken is in a crouching state from 4th landing recovery frame onward

rows.name: Dragonlash Kick
rows.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 32; first 3 active frames only hit airborne and do 1000 damage, causing spike knockdown on hit or OTG bounce on Punish Counter; hits standing and crouching opponents on 4th active frame onward, dealing 950 damage; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 32; first 3 active frames only hit airborne and do 1000 damage, causing spike knockdown on hit or OTG bounce on Punish Counter; hits standing and crouching opponents on 4th active frame onward, dealing 950 damage; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Dragonlash Kick
rows.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 37; first 2 active frames only hit airborne, causing spike knockdown on hit or OTG bounce on Punish Counter; 1f better hit/block advantage vs. crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 37; first 2 active frames only hit airborne, causing spike knockdown on hit or OTG bounce on Punish Counter; 1f better hit/block advantage vs. crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Dragonlash Kick
rows.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 40; first 2 active frames only hit airborne, causing spike knockdown on hit or OTG bounce on Punish Counter; 1f better hit/block advantage vs. crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 40; first 2 active frames only hit airborne, causing spike knockdown on hit or OTG bounce on Punish Counter; 1f better hit/block advantage vs. crouching opponents; cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Dragonlash Kick
rows.damage_scaling: Combo (5% extra); both hits apply scaling
rows.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 32; initial active frames 2(7)1 only hit airborne opponents for an OTG bounce and side switch; next 3 active frames only hit standing opponents; 3f better hit/block advantage vs. crouching opponents (allows link into 2LP); cannot hit cross-up

raw.dmgScaling: Combo (5% extra); both hits apply scaling
raw.notes: Can dodge lows until frame 32; initial active frames 2(7)1 only hit airborne opponents for an OTG bounce and side switch; next 3 active frames only hit standing opponents; 3f better hit/block advantage vs. crouching opponents (allows link into 2LP); cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Jinrai Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be delayed up to 3f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); follow-ups can also be input on whiff

raw.notes: Follow-up can be delayed up to 3f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); follow-ups can also be input on whiff

rows.name: Jinrai Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be delayed up to 2f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); best starter for frame trapping into 6LK follow-up; follow-ups can also be input on whiff

raw.notes: Follow-up can be delayed up to 2f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); best starter for frame trapping into 6LK follow-up; follow-ups can also be input on whiff

rows.name: Jinrai Kick
rows.notes: Puts opponents into limited juggle state; follow-up can be delayed up to 5f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); follow-ups can also be input on whiff; good approach tool that doesn't extend a hurtbox until active

raw.notes: Puts opponents into limited juggle state; follow-up can be delayed up to 5f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); follow-ups can also be input on whiff; good approach tool that doesn't extend a hurtbox until active

rows.name: Kazekama Shin Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; using 236MK~6LK will counter-hit any opponent button; causes a +32 Hard Knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (gives good oki but prevents follow-up links)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; using 236MK~6LK will counter-hit any opponent button; causes a +32 Hard Knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (gives good oki but prevents follow-up links)

rows.name: Gorai Axe Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 4-21, allowing it to dodge some low attacks; due to its slow startup, opponent can easily switch from low block to high block to cover both mixup options; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG bounce state (can only follow up with SA3 cancel if the juggle was started with the 6MK follow-up)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 4-21, allowing it to dodge some low attacks; due to its slow startup, opponent can easily switch from low block to high block to cover both mixup options; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG bounce state (can only follow up with SA3 cancel if the juggle was started with the 6MK follow-up)

rows.name: Senka Snap Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; better KD Adv. after 236HK launch; whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox (236LK -23/-35; 236MK -22/-31; 236HK KD/-26)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; better KD Adv. after 236HK launch; whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox (236LK {{sf6-adv|VM|-23/-35}}; 236MK {{sf6-adv|VM|-22/-31}}; 236HK KD/{{sf6-adv|VM|-26}})

rows.name: Jinrai Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be delayed up to 6f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); follow-ups can also be input on whiff

raw.notes: Follow-up can be delayed up to 6f if input as late as possible (affecting frame trap and juggle opportunities); follow-ups can also be input on whiff

rows.name: Kazekama Shin Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; +7 Punish Counter only possible if first hit whiffs; always a true blockstring even if delayed

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; +7 Punish Counter only possible if first hit whiffs; always a true blockstring even if delayed

rows.name: Gorai Axe Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 4f gap between hits on block, good for setting up trade combos (up to 10f gap if delayed); causes OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 5-18, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 4f gap between hits on block, good for setting up trade combos (up to 10f gap if delayed); causes OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 5-18, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

rows.name: Senka Snap Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; whiffs on crouching opponents (-18 vs. crouch block); true blockstring vs. stand block, can be delayed to create up to 3f gap or to connect on high-juggled opponents

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; whiffs on crouching opponents (-18 vs. crouch block); true blockstring vs. stand block, can be delayed to create up to 3f gap or to connect on high-juggled opponents

rows.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (LK)
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 2f gap between final 2 hits on block; (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 2f gap between final 2 hits on block; (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

rows.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (MK)
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; final 2 hits are a true blockstring; (refers to scaled damage)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; final 2 hits are a true blockstring; (refers to scaled damage)

rows.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (HK)
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; only combos on standing opponents because the 6HK whiffs on crouchers; 7f gap between final 2 hits on stand block (20f if 2nd hit whiffs vs. crouching); (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; only combos on standing opponents because the 6HK whiffs on crouchers; 7f gap between final 2 hits on stand block (20f if 2nd hit whiffs vs. crouching); (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

rows.name: Quick Dash
rows.notes: Can input follow-ups from 11-31f; follow-ups will not reverse direction if crossing under opponent; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration; when inputting with 5KKK takes priority over OD special moves (does not apply when inputting with 5KK after performing a special move input); Run Distance: 1.37 (early stop) / 2.92 (max)

raw.notes: Can input follow-ups from 11-31f; follow-ups will not reverse direction if crossing under opponent; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration; when inputting with 5KKK takes priority over OD special moves (does not apply when inputting with 5KK after performing a special move input); Run Distance: 1.37 (early stop) / 2.92 (max)

rows.name: Emergency Stop
rows.notes: 27f is fastest total Run~Stop time; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration

raw.notes: 27f is fastest total Run~Stop time; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration

rows.name: Thunder Kick
rows.notes: Ken's only overhead outside of Jinrai Kick follow-ups; notably can't benefit from Drive Rush like other overheads, but can be used in meaty setups (+4) to link afterwards; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 10-20, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

raw.notes: Ken's only overhead outside of Jinrai Kick follow-ups; notably can't benefit from Drive Rush like other overheads, but can be used in meaty setups (+4) to link afterwards; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 10-20, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

rows.name: Forward Step Kick
rows.notes: Acts like a Normal Attack for Super canceling (including no extra damage scaling when canceled to SA3); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; can be spaced safely in neutral, or used as a frame trap out of 5HP~Run (allowing a link on counter-hit); extends a forward hurtbox during recovery

raw.notes: Acts like a Normal Attack for Super canceling (including no extra damage scaling when canceled to SA3); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; can be spaced safely in neutral, or used as a frame trap out of 5HP~Run (allowing a link on counter-hit); extends a forward hurtbox during recovery

rows.name: Run~Shoryuken
rows.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; final hit whiffs vs. crouch block (1f worse advantage, 75 less chip dmg); cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; final hit whiffs vs. crouch block (1f worse advantage, 75 less chip dmg); cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Run~Tatsumaki Senpukyaku
rows.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; alternates between forward/backward hits; backwards hitbox can pull opponent towards Ken from behind; CH/PC advantage carries through all hits, making it a great punish starter against very unsafe moves; leaves Ken at close range for a strike/throw mixup on grounded hits; () refers to knockdown advantage when canceled from 5MP~HP

raw.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; alternates between forward/backward hits; backwards hitbox can pull opponent towards Ken from behind; CH/PC advantage carries through all hits, making it a great punish starter against very unsafe moves; leaves Ken at close range for a strike/throw mixup on grounded hits; () refers to knockdown advantage when canceled from 5MP~HP

rows.name: Run~Dragonlash Kick
rows.damage_scaling: Combo (5% extra); both hits apply scaling
rows.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; properties similar to 623KK (startup, hits twice in juggles, side switch, 2f better advantage vs. crouch); lower OTG bounce height than 623KK; functional startup vs. standing opponents is 11+20f

raw.dmgScaling: Combo (5% extra); both hits apply scaling

rows.name: Dragonlash Flame
rows.notes: Switches sides with opponent on hit; Full Dmg distribution: 500,300x2,900

raw.notes: Switches sides with opponent on hit; Full Dmg distribution: 500,300x2,900

rows.name: Shippu Jinrai-kyaku
rows.notes: Fast startup and good range, making it good for punishes and juggle combos; completely safe on block due to pushback; great chip tool vs. opponents in Burnout (can continue dealing more chip after being safely blocked); benefits from 20% Counter-hit/Punish Counter damage increase unlike most Supers; Full Dmg distribution: 300x4,200x5,600

raw.notes: Fast startup and good range, making it good for punishes and juggle combos; completely safe on block due to pushback; great chip tool vs. opponents in Burnout (can continue dealing more chip after being safely blocked); benefits from 20% Counter-hit/Punish Counter damage increase unlike most Supers; Full Dmg distribution: 300x4,200x5,600

rows.name: Shinryu Reppa
rows.notes: 1st hit leads to full animation (non-cinematic damage varies based on juggle height, and allows a follow-up juggle); 2 hits whiff on crouch block (reduced chip, slightly worse advantage); cinematic time regenerates ~2 Drive bars for Ken
